developer.comment: Amsterdam-based company; raised $8M seed in Dec 2025.

first-release.comment: Project began as a fork of Roo Code in late 2025; GA/relaunch shipped April 2, 2026. Earliest extension releases predate GA (the team forked Roo Code and shipped immediately).

status.comment: Actively developed; 400+ releases, latest v7.3.x as of June 2026.

license.comment: MIT License on the GitHub repo (some marketing pages say Apache-2.0; the repo LICENSE file is MIT). Either way OSI-approved open source.

specialization.comment: Broad agentic coding agent; modes for plan/code/debug but not niche-specialized.

interface.comment: VS Code & JetBrains extensions, a CLI, plus cloud/web agents and Slack.

host-ides.comment: VS Code and JetBrains (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm) extensions; CLI runs in terminal; cloud agents in browser.

open-source.comment: Extension source is MIT-licensed and public on GitHub.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Primary form is a VS Code/JetBrains extension; also a CLI and cloud agents.

default-model-provider.comment: Model-agnostic; 500+ models via Kilo Gateway and 20+ BYOK providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, DeepSeek, etc.). No single locked default.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK across 20+ providers at zero markup.

local-model-support.comment: Supports Ollama and LM Studio for fully local models.

multi-file-editing.comment: Coordinated edits across the codebase; inherits Cline/Roo multi-file agent loop.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agentic loop edits, runs commands, and iterates; Orchestrator/Architect modes chain steps toward a goal.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs terminal/shell commands as part of its loop.

mcp-support.comment: Documented MCP support with an MCP server marketplace.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Orchestrator mode delegates subtasks to specialized modes/agents; cloud agents can run in parallel.

codebase-indexing.comment: Codebase semantic search/indexing supported (embeddings-based code index, Cline/Roo lineage).

planning-mode.comment: Architect mode is an explicit plan-before-code phase.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input (screenshots/mockups), inherited from Cline/Roo vision support.

browser-computer-use.comment: Browser automation / control (KiloClaw and Cline-lineage browser tool) to navigate and verify changes.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Model-driven: full-tool-access agents (Code/Plan/Debug) decide on their own to launch subagents mid-task via the task/new_task tool when a subtask would benefit from isolation, without the user explicitly invoking them. (The older user-switched Orchestrator mode is now deprecated in favor of this native automatic delegation.) Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Chat-driven edits with approval, autonomous multi-step runs, plus async cloud agents. Inline completion is also offered (see inline-completion attribute).

inline-completion.comment: Ships tab-style inline autocomplete alongside the agent.

background-async-execution.comment: Cloud agents and scheduled/cron tasks run detached from the editor session.

persistent-memory.comment: Memory Bank feature plus persistent project rules carry context across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Built-in checkpoints to restore the workspace before an agent run.

pr-creation.comment: Cloud agents and code-review/deploy workflows can open PRs.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md and .kilocode/rules/ project instruction files.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Custom modes, workflows (slash commands), and MCP servers extend behavior.

permission-model.comment: Per-action approval by default with configurable auto-approve (YOLO/auto-run) settings, Cline/Roo lineage.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open-source extension running fully local models keeps code on-machine; no managed cloud required. No formal on-prem enterprise server product documented.

privacy-mode.comment: Vendor states code is not used for model training; local-model mode keeps code on-device.

enterprise-controls.comment: Enterprise tier lists SSO/OIDC, SCIM, and audit logs. RBAC/VPC not explicitly confirmed; SCIM implies user provisioning.

pricing-model.comment: Open-source extension is free; Kilo Pass token subscriptions, pay-as-you-go credits, and zero-markup BYOK all offered. Checked 2026-06-05.

free-tier.comment: Free & open-source extension; free credits to start, and BYOK avoids vendor billing entirely.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Core agent is free and open-source; usable via BYOK or free credits without a paid plan. Cheapest token subscription (Kilo Pass Starter) is $19/mo if desired. Checked 2026-06-05.

maturity.comment: 3M+ users, ~20k GitHub stars, mature Cline/Roo lineage; very rapid release cadence still evolving, so not a full 5.

community-size.comment: 3M+ downloads and ~20k stars; large but below Copilot/Cursor scale. Checked 2026-06-05.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP server marketplace, community rules/workflows, and custom modes.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md and the vendor-specific .kilocode/rules/ files (also backward-compatible with .roo/.clinerules conventions).

memory-scopes.comment: Global (user) rules, project-root rules, directory-scoped AGENTS.md/.kilocode/rules per subfolder; enterprise/Teams policy at org level.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Directory/path-scoped rule files (nested AGENTS.md and .kilocode/rules) plus always-on root rules; mode-specific rules also supported.

auto-memory.comment: Memory Bank is user/agent-initiated documentation rather than automatic cross-session memory the agent writes on its own; no autonomous auto-memory writer documented. Checked 2026-06-05.

skill-support.comment: Workflows are reusable, on-demand slash-command recipes (markdown templates) beyond plain rules files.

skill-invocation.comment: Workflows invoked explicitly by the user via /workflow-name slash commands.

always-on-surface.comment: Workflow bodies are pulled in on invocation; only the command name is referenced by default (progressive disclosure).

skill-distribution.comment: Workflows are markdown files placed in .kilocode/workflows / .kilo/commands; shared via git/manual copy. No dedicated workflow marketplace.

sandboxing-technology.comment: The VS Code/JetBrains extension and CLI run commands directly in the user's shell with full user privileges; safety is via approval prompts and allow/deny rules, not kernel/container isolation. No documented OS-sandbox, container, or VM isolation for local execution (Cline/Roo lineage). Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Per-tool auto-approve toggles (read/write/execute/browser/MCP); terminal command-prefix allowlist/denylist (e.g. git, npm, *); per-path glob rules in kilo.jsonc; reads and edits/writes are gated separately. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: kilo.jsonc permission rules use last-matching-rule-wins glob evaluation, i.e. user-controlled evaluation order, allowing later patterns to re-override earlier ones. A separate command denylist always overrides the allowlist (deny-wins) for terminal commands. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: User authors allow/ask/deny glob rules per tool in kilo.jsonc plus command allow/deny lists. No documented programmable hook/policy-script decision point that inspects each action in code; highest level reached is declarative-rules. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: Reading .env triggers an approval prompt by default, and *.env.local / *.env.production also prompt while *.env.example is pre-allowed — built-in out-of-the-box gating without a user-authored rule. (Note: arbitrary custom secret paths still need user-defined deny rules.) Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: Auto-approve toggles and command allow/deny lists are written to kilo.jsonc config and reflected across UI and CLI, surviving restarts. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: YOLO ('You Only Live Once') mode is a master toggle that auto-approves all actions — reading, writing, and executing commands — for hands-off autonomous runs. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: No network firewall/allowlist for the agent's own execution; outbound is unrestricted by default since commands run with full user privileges. Network/HTTP access can be gated indirectly via per-tool command and MCP allow/deny rules (e.g. denying curl/fetch or specific MCP tools), so treated as configurable rather than a dedicated egress control. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box, auto-approve is off and Kilo asks before edits and command execution (default fallback is 'ask' when no rule matches); the user opts into auto-approve/YOLO. Checked 2026-06-12.
